content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as inherently curiosity-shaped, recursive agent structures where the agent itself modulates which new memory traces get encoded and retained based on intrinsic curiosity signals, rather than just memory being a passive storage module for the agent. This recursive framing means the memory system acts as a persistent, low-level 'curiosity co-agent' that drives future active learning selection independently of the agent’s high-level task policy.
